current_state.spec_says: data-model.md and contracts/role-defaults.md still describe public role constants and the old UserPolicyOverrides merge signature.
current_state.code_does: The implementation uses private default constructors, RoleDefaultPolicy::for_role(), and EffectivePolicy::merge(role, Vec<String>).

proposed_resolution: Remove public SERVICE_DEFAULT, WORKER_DEFAULT, JOB_DEFAULT, SIDECAR_DEFAULT, and SUPERVISOR_DEFAULT constant descriptions.
Describe RoleDefaultPolicy::for_role() as the public lookup API over private role default constructors.
Replace the old UserPolicyOverrides merge signature with Vec<String> override field markers.
Separate missing work_role fallback from unknown work_role configuration errors in the data flow.
proposals.rationale: The tested code is authoritative. Public constants cannot express the complete role default values that include Vec fields without misleading users.

current_state.spec_says: Missing or unknown work_role values both fall back to the documented conservative default.
current_state.code_does: Missing work_role falls back to Worker with WARN diagnostics. Unknown work_role strings are rejected by strict serde enum deserialization.

proposed_resolution: Update the spec edge case so missing work_role falls back to Worker.
Define unknown work_role strings as fatal configuration errors with readable diagnostics.
proposals.rationale: Rejecting unknown role strings prevents configuration typos from silently changing runtime behavior.

current_state.code_does: RoleDefaultPolicy contains internal success_exit_codes, but ChildSpec has no external override field and TaskExit does not carry raw process exit codes.

proposed_resolution: State that current success semantics are driven by TaskResult::Succeeded or equivalent typed success facts.
Clarify that RoleDefaultPolicy.success_exit_codes is internal policy data in this slice.
Move ChildSpec.success_exit_codes user override support out of the current contract.
proposals.rationale: The runtime does not currently expose raw process exit codes to the policy pipeline. Advertising a ChildSpec override would be a false capability.
